title: Multiple sclerosis (Lancet Seminar)
rationale: Foundational field-wide overview establishing RRMS/SPMS/PPMS taxonomy and diagnostic vocabulary prerequisite to all other topics; large forward citation neighborhood bridges 2008–2024 literature

title: Diagnosis of MS: 2017 revisions of McDonald Criteria
rationale: Current diagnostic standard; McDonald criteria are the operational definition of MS; every diagnostic and monitoring paper refers to this consensus

title: Relapses and progression of disability in MS (Lyon cohort)
rationale: Established that disability progression proceeds independently of relapses; conceptual foundation of progressive MS research agenda; Lyon cohort NEJM paper is the field's baseline for DMT trial effect interpretation

title: Natural history of MS: a unifying concept
rationale: Unifying natural history model; companion to NEJM 2000 Lyon cohort; establishes age-dependent progression floor independent of phenotype; generates large foundational citation neighborhood

title: Multiple sclerosis pathology
rationale: Authoritative pathology synthesis from the leading histopathologist in MS; demyelination lesion heterogeneity; axonal loss; shadow plaques; essential for understanding why imaging correlates imperfectly with disability

title: MS: an immune or neurodegenerative disorder?
rationale: Foundational framing of the immune-vs-neurodegeneration duality; explains why peripheral immunosuppression fails to prevent progressive disability; Trapp axonal damage work is widely cited across the entire pathophysiology literature

title: MS genomic map (IMSGC GWAS)
rationale: Largest MS GWAS to date; 233 risk variants; establishes polygenic architecture and HLA dominance; anchor for all genetic epidemiology papers in the corpus

title: Genetics of MS: overview and new directions
rationale: Mechanistic synthesis of genetic architecture; explains HLA-DRB1 and non-HLA risk; translates GWAS findings into biological pathways; complements IMSGC GWAS with interpretive depth

title: EBV longitudinal analysis and MS
rationale: Landmark US military cohort establishing causal EBV-MS relationship; 10 million military serum samples; most cited MS risk factor paper since 2020; generates large forward citation pool for emerging etiology literature

title: Env risk factors for MS Pt I: infection
rationale: Comprehensive risk factor synthesis covering EBV, vitamin D, smoking, latitude; foundational framing of multi-factor etiology model; Ascherio group generates large citation neighborhood for environmental epidemiology subfield

title: 2021 MAGNIMS-CMSC-NAIMS MRI consensus
rationale: Current MRI monitoring consensus; covers activity monitoring, treatment response, PML surveillance; widely cited in clinical trial protocols and monitoring guidelines; expands into large imaging and biomarker literature

title: Chronic active MS lesions and disability in vivo
rationale: In vivo paramagnetic rim lesion (PRL) validation; connects imaging biomarkers to smoldering disease mechanism; bridges TOPIC-05 and TOPIC-08; central vein sign literature anchor

title: Neurofilaments as biomarkers in neurological disorders
rationale: Foundational neurofilament light chain (NfL) review; establishes serum NfL as cross-disease biomarker; cited by virtually every subsequent NfL paper; generates very large forward citation neighborhood covering biomarker subfield

title: Potential of serum NfL as biomarker for MS
selection_source: Seed — Topic 6B (retired v1.3 — GFAP and McDonald 2024 seeds added; NfL overlap with MS-T1-013)
rationale: MS-specific serum NfL synthesis; covers treatment monitoring and prognostic applications; bridges NfL biology (TOPIC-06) with DMT monitoring (TOPIC-07) and progressive MS (TOPIC-08); complements Khalil 2018 with MS focus

title: Ocrelizumab vs IFN beta-1a in relapsing MS (OPERA)
rationale: Pivotal B-cell depletion trial in RRMS; established ocrelizumab standard and anti-CD20 paradigm; most-cited recent MS RCT; generates large forward citation neighborhood covering all subsequent B-cell and DMT papers

title: Ocrelizumab vs placebo in primary progressive MS (ORATORIO)
rationale: Only approved DMT for PPMS; proof-of-concept for B-cell depletion in progressive MS; redistributed to TOPIC-08 to avoid anti-CD20 seed duplication in TOPIC-07; widely cited in progressive MS treatment and PIRA literature

title: Defining clinical course of MS: 2013 revisions
rationale: Current phenotype classification consensus; RRMS/SPMS/PPMS/CIS/RIS definitions used uniformly in clinical trials and clinical care; every phenotype assignment in the corpus references this standard

title: Smouldering multiple sclerosis: the real MS
rationale: Smoldering MS conceptual framework; introduces PIRA as primary disability driver; argues that relapse suppression is necessary but not sufficient; generates forward citations in progressive MS and BTK inhibitor literature

title: Patient-reported outcomes in MS care
rationale: PRO instrument landscape including fatigue, cognition, QoL; highlights EDSS limitations; entering aging territory but still widely cited as PRO framework reference; expands into trial endpoint and patient measurement literature

title: Evaluation of MS disability outcome measures (MSOAC)
rationale: MSOAC consortium outcome measure analysis; systematic evaluation of composite endpoints in progressive MS trials; links PRO research to trial design and regulatory context

title: Incidence/prevalence of psychiatric disorders in MS
rationale: First systematic review of psychiatric comorbidity burden in MS; Marrie group's most-cited comorbidity paper; generates citation neighborhood for the entire comorbidity subfield; aging but foundational

title: Cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis
rationale: Foundational cognitive impairment synthesis; processing speed and memory as primary domains affected; still widely cited as the baseline cognitive neuroscience reference for MS; generates forward citations across neuropsychology subfield

title: Rate of pregnancy-related relapse in MS (PRIMS)
rationale: PRIMS cohort established the pregnancy relapse trajectory (protective during 3rd trimester; rebound post-partum); cited by every subsequent pregnancy/MS paper; generates large foundational citation neighborhood for reproductive MS literature

title: Family planning considerations in MS
rationale: Current clinical guidance on DMT use in pregnancy; washout periods; teratogenicity; breastfeeding; most recent comprehensive consensus replacing 2015-era guidance; expands into post-partum, fertility, and DMT safety literature

title: IPMSSG criteria for pediatric MS (2013 revisions)
rationale: International Pediatric MS Study Group criteria; distinguishes pediatric MS from ADEM and other demyelinating diseases; standard diagnostic reference for all pediatric MS literature; generates pediatric-specific citation neighborhood

title: Trial of fingolimod vs IFN beta-1a in pediatric MS (PARADIGMS)
rationale: PARADIGMS is the pivotal pediatric MS trial; only head-to-head DMT RCT in pediatric population; establishes pediatric-specific evidence base; EBV and neurodevelopment literature emerges from this population

title: The prevalence of multiple sclerosis in the United States by race and ethnicity
rationale: Population-based US prevalence estimate by race/ethnicity; empirically anchors disparities epidemiology with a strong citation neighborhood and direct relevance to diagnostic equity and treatment access

title: Racial and ethnic disparities in MS prevalence
rationale: Large-scale Southern California MS cohort establishing higher prevalence in Black and Hispanic populations vs historical white-dominant estimates; empirical complement to structural racism review; expands into disparities epidemiology literature

title: Artificial intelligence and multiple sclerosis
rationale: Current AI applications review covering lesion segmentation, progression prediction, treatment response; addresses implementation gap and explainability; expands into clinical AI and digital health literature

title: Integrating big data and AI to predict MS progression
rationale: 2025 synthesis of multimodal data integration for progression prediction; covers wearables, EHR, imaging, and CSF biomarkers as inputs to ML models; bridges AI (TOPIC-14) and biomarkers (TOPIC-06) and progressive MS (TOPIC-08)

title: AI-driven reclassification of MS progression
rationale: 2025 data-driven MS reclassification challenging traditional phenotypes; Nature Medicine publication signals field-level significance; bridges AI/ML methods (TOPIC-14) and disease categorization (TOPIC-01/TOPIC-08); generates emerging frontiers citation neighborhood

title: Remyelination in CNS: from biology to therapy
rationale: Foundational remyelination review; oligodendrocyte precursor biology; remyelination failure in chronic MS; generates forward citation neighborhood for all remyelination and neuroprotection therapeutic target papers

title: Pathways to cures for MS: research roadmap 2022
selection_source: Framing Seed R7 — Promoted from Topic 16A
rationale: NMSS Stop/Restore/End framework incorporating input from 300+ people living with MS; endorsed by 30+ MS societies; reference list is a high-quality expansion signal for foundational papers across progressive MS, biomarkers, and equity; promoted to framing anchor R7 in v1.3

title: Refined Pathways to Cures Research Roadmap 2024
selection_source: Framing Seed R8 — Promoted from Topic 16B
rationale: 2024 update to Cures Roadmap; multi-regional authorship; incorporates smoldering MS and remyelination evidence; promoted to framing anchor R8 in v1.3; reference list adds recency signal for frontier papers post-2022

title: Rising prevalence of MS: Atlas of MS 3rd edition
rationale: MSIF global prevalence atlas; 2.8 million people with MS worldwide; geographic distribution including coverage gaps in Africa and Global South; primary quantitative frame for epidemiology topic and global burden discussion

title: Global burden of MS 1990-2016 (GBD 2016)
rationale: GBD systematic review of MS burden across 195 countries; incidence, prevalence, DALYs, mortality trends; Lancet Neurology anchor generating large epidemiology and global health citation neighborhood

title: Validity of the Symbol Digit Modalities Test as a cognition performance outcome measure for multiple sclerosis
rationale: MS-specific validation of SDMT as a cognitive performance endpoint; high structural centrality in outcome-measure literature and direct bridge between cognition, PROs, and trial endpoint design

title: The Multiple Sclerosis Impact Scale (MSIS-29): a new patient-based outcome measure
rationale: Foundational patient-reported impact instrument used across MS studies; provides deep historical citation linkage for fatigue and quality-of-life outcome measurement

title: The incidence of multiple sclerosis in multiple racial and ethnic groups
rationale: Canonical incidence study across racial/ethnic groups; complements prevalence-focused seeds and strengthens TOPIC-13 structural connectivity through an established citation neighborhood

title: Progressive multiple sclerosis: prospects for disease therapy, repair, and restoration of function
rationale: Progressive MS research agenda including disease biology, trial strategy, and repair; redistributed from TOPIC-16 to TOPIC-08 in v1.3; Lancet Neurology bridge across progressive disease and repair literature

title: Association of Disease Severity and Socioeconomic Status in Black and White Americans With Multiple Sclerosis
rationale: Documents persistent disease-severity disparities between Black and White Americans with MS independent of race; links SES as a mediating factor; bridges structural racism and clinical outcomes; complements Langer-Gould 2022 (MS-T1-028) prevalence data with severity and SES outcomes evidence

title: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial of natalizumab for relapsing multiple sclerosis
rationale: AFFIRM trial; established natalizumab as first highly effective DMT; VLA-4 integrin mechanism distinct from anti-CD20; citation neighborhood covers PML/JC risk management, treatment escalation vs treat-to-target debate; ~3000 citations

title: Siponimod versus placebo in secondary progressive multiple sclerosis (EXPAND)
rationale: EXPAND trial; first S1P modulator to slow disability accumulation in SPMS; bridges TOPIC-07 (DMTs) and TOPIC-08 (progressive MS); covers distinct SPMS-specific treatment evidence neighborhood; siponimod and S1P modulator literature

title: Blood GFAP as an emerging biomarker in brain and spinal cord disorders
rationale: GFAP cross-disease review; complements NfL (MS-T1-013) by anchoring astrocyte-injury biomarker literature; opens GFAP-specific citation neighborhood not reachable from NfL-focused seeds; Nat Rev Neurol anchor for emerging fluid biomarker panel literature

title: Diagnosis of MS: 2024 revisions of McDonald criteria
authors: Montalban X, Oh J, Banwell B et al.
selection_source: Seed augmentation — Topic 6D (also Framing Seed R6)
rationale: 2024 McDonald criteria; formally incorporates serum biomarkers into diagnostic algorithm; bridges TOPIC-05 and TOPIC-06; establishes clinical thresholds for NfL and kappa free light chains in diagnosis; authoritative biomarker criteria document

title: Advancing care and outcomes for African American patients with multiple sclerosis
rationale: DMT access and clinical outcomes for African American MS patients; fills DMT access disparity gap absent from prevalence/incidence-focused equity seeds; generates distinct citation neighborhood covering access barriers, treatment uptake, and outcomes by race

title: Exercise as medicine in multiple sclerosis — time to act
rationale: JAMA Neurology anchor for exercise-as-treatment literature; opens rehabilitation and exercise intervention neighborhood for TOPIC-16; cross-domain coverage of fatigue, cognition, and mood outcomes of exercise

title: Rehabilitation in multiple sclerosis: a systematic review of systematic reviews
rationale: Umbrella review of rehabilitation evidence in MS; covers fatigue, cognition, mobility, pain management, spasticity; JLA patient priority for multidisciplinary care; broadest expansion anchor for non-DMT symptom management literature

title: Remyelination in multiple sclerosis: from basic science to clinical translation
rationale: Clinical translation bridge from remyelination biology (Franklin 2008) to therapeutic pipeline; Lancet Neurology 2020 anchor for remyelination-as-treatment literature; neuroprotection and repair strategy neighborhood
